title: store::drain uses id<=i64::MAX when batch empty — silently deletes newly enqueued messages
description: When drain() fetches 0 non-expired rows, rows.last() is None → bind value i64::MAX. The DELETE WHERE to_pub_id=?1 AND (expires_at<=now OR id<=i64::MAX) fires atomically with the SELECT but within the same transaction. If a new message is enqueued for this user between the SELECT returning empty and the DELETE executing, the new message has an autoincrement id that satisfies id<=i64::MAX and is deleted without being delivered to the client. Trigger: concurrent enqueue (e.g. a WHISPER arriving during drain) when the drain SELECT batch returns empty. Actual bad behavior: message silently dropped, client never receives it, sender gets ok=true.
